title: Auckland in June
meta_desc: Plan a family holiday in Auckland this June with our guide. Discover what's on, weather, crowd levels and tips for a fun-filled trip with kids in NZ's biggest city
intro: June is a great time to visit Auckland with kids, with mild winter weather and a range of exciting activities to enjoy. As a family, you can explore the city's many beaches, visit the famous Auckland Zoo, or take a ferry to Waiheke Island. With a budget of $200-$350 per day for a family of four, you can experience the best of Auckland without breaking the bank.

sections.heading: Weather in June
sections.body:
In June, Auckland's weather is mild, with average highs of 14°C and lows of 8°C. It's a great time to enjoy the city's many outdoor activities, such as visiting the beach or going for a hike. However, it's also a good idea to pack warm clothing, as the temperature can drop significantly at night.

June is also a relatively dry month in Auckland, with an average of 120mm of rainfall. This makes it an ideal time to visit the city's many parks and gardens, such as Albert Park and the Auckland Botanic Gardens.
list: Average high: 14°C
Average low: 8°C
Average rainfall: 120mm

sections.heading: What's On in June
sections.body:
June is a great time to visit Auckland, with a range of exciting events and activities to enjoy. The Auckland Arts Festival takes place in June, featuring a range of performances, exhibitions, and installations. The festival is a great way to experience the city's vibrant arts scene, with something for all ages to enjoy.

The Sky Tower also offers a range of activities and events in June, including a winter lights display and a ice skating rink. The tower's observation deck offers stunning views of the city, and is a must-visit attraction for families.
list: Auckland Arts Festival
Sky Tower winter lights display
Ice skating rink at the Sky Tower

sections.heading: Best Activities for June
sections.body:
June is a great time to visit some of Auckland's most popular attractions, such as the Auckland Zoo and Kelly Tarlton's SEA LIFE Aquarium. The zoo is home to over 1,400 animals, including elephants, giraffes, and monkeys, while the aquarium features a range of marine life, including sharks, stingrays, and penguins.

The Sky Tower observation deck is also a must-visit attraction, offering stunning views of the city and harbour. And for a fun and active day out, consider taking a ferry to Waiheke Island or going for a hike on Rangitoto Island.
list: Auckland Zoo: $25-$35 per person
Kelly Tarlton's SEA LIFE Aquarium: $30-$40 per person
Sky Tower observation deck: $32-$42 per person
Ferry to Waiheke Island: $40-$60 per person, return
Rangitoto Island hike: free, but ferry costs $30-$40 per person, return

sections.heading: Best Indoor Activities for June
sections.body: June can bring the odd cold, wet day, so it pays to have weather-proof options ready. Auckland has the widest range of indoor attractions in the country. MOTAT (the Museum of Transport and Technology) is a hands-on favourite spanning two sites linked by a vintage tram, perfect for a half- or full day. The Auckland War Memorial Museum is free for residents (donation for visitors) and has a brilliant kids' discovery centre. The Stardome Observatory in One Tree Hill runs family planetarium shows, and the Sky Tower's observation deck is a fun, weather-proof outing with city-wide views. Add Kelly Tarlton's aquarium and a trampoline park or two, and you'll never be stuck for a rainy-day plan.
list: MOTAT — hands-on transport and tech museum across two tram-linked sites
Auckland War Memorial Museum — free for residents, with a kids' discovery centre
Stardome Observatory — family planetarium shows in One Tree Hill
Sky Tower observation deck — weather-proof views over the whole city
Kelly Tarlton's aquarium and indoor trampoline parks for active days

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body:
When visiting Auckland with kids, it's a good idea to plan ahead and book popular attractions in advance. This can help avoid crowds and long wait times, and ensure that you make the most of your time in the city.

It's also a good idea to consider the weather when planning your activities, and to pack warm clothing for cooler mornings and evenings. And don't forget to take advantage of the city's many free activities, such as visiting the beach or going for a walk in one of the many parks and gardens.
list: Book popular attractions in advance
Pack warm clothing for cooler mornings and evenings
Take advantage of free activities, such as visiting the beach or going for a walk
Consider purchasing a family pass for popular attractions, which can offer discounts and convenience
Don't forget to check the weather forecast before heading out each day
